sections.label: Pallavi
sections.ta:
சிவா என்றழைத்தாலே தேனூறும்
அவாவுடன் ஹ்ருதய பூர்வமாய் சதா
sections.translit:
sivA endRazhaitthAlE thEnURum
avAvudan hrudhaya pUrvamAy sadhA
sections.en: If one with a heartfelt desire calls out the name of Lord Siva, it is certain that nectar will start gushing into one's mouth.

sections.label: Anupallavi
sections.ta:
மனம் குழைந்து ஸ்வர ஞானம் தன்னோடு
தினம் இழைந்து அந்த மயிலை வாசனை
sections.translit:
manam kuzhaindhu swara nyAnam thannOdu
dhinam izhaindhu andha mayilai vAsanai
sections.en: If with a melted heart and mind and with perfect unison and melody, one addresses the Lord who resides in Mylapore as Siva, nectar will start gushing into one's mouth.

sections.label: Charanam
sections.ta:
பால் நீறணிந்த பரமன் பங்கஜ பாதன்
பாலனை காக்க காலனை உதைத்த ஜாலன்
பஞ்சாமிர்தம் கெஞ்சாமலே கிடைத்ததுபோல்
அஞ்செழுத்து மந்திரத்தை நெஞ்சில் வைத்து
sections.translit:
pAl nIRaNindha paraman pangkaja pAdhan
bAlanai kAkka kAlanai udhaittha jAlan
panjchAmirdham kenjAmalE kidaitthadhupOl
anjezhutthu mandhiratthai nenjil vaitthu
sections.en: He is the God adorning pure white sacred ash and with tender lotus feet. He is a trickster who kicked even the God of death, Yama, to protect a child, Markandeya. When one starts chanting the five letters in the name of the Lord Namasivaya with a deep love in one's heart, the feeling is utterly unbelievable. It is as if the delicate mix of plantain, honey, sugar, ghee, and grapes was offered to you on your tongue without asking for it. Author’s Note Second line in the charanam refers to the legend of Siva kicking the God of death, Yama, in Thirukkadaiyur to save the young Markandeya. Siva kicks Yama
